summary.what_happened: Checkout latency saturated near the tax-api client timeout and error rate rose to ~6% starting 15:53; the incident is ongoing.
summary.customer_impact: A meaningful share of checkouts fail or take over 5 seconds.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-20T16:10:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

severity.explanation: checkout error rate 6.1% (>1% on a revenue-critical flow) with p95 latency pinned near the 5000ms client timeout, ongoing at window end

evidence.interpretation: the only change in the package is a routine payments-service deploy on 06-15, five days before onset with no checkout-path changes - timing misaligned, internal change ruled out as the trigger
first slow tax-api warning at 15:53:41 (1840ms vs ~90ms p50), escalating to 5000ms TimeoutErrors by 15:57
circuit breaker for tax-api opened at 16:08:55 after 12 consecutive timeouts and never closed; probes keep timing out
card authorization path reported healthy at 16:29 (charge_card p95 182ms)
payments p95 rose 380ms -> 5400ms and plateaued at the 5000ms client timeout ceiling, first crossing 2x baseline at 15:55
tax-api p95 pinned at exactly 5000 from 16:05 onward - a flat ceiling characteristic of client-side timeout truncation, not load
checkout-db CPU stayed at baseline (41-46%), ruling out the database
runbook failure mode 'tax-api degradation (third party)' matches: spans pinned at the 5000ms timeout, circuit breaker opening, healthy card path
runbook explicitly warns against harder retries during vendor timeouts (retry amplification)
quote_tax accounts for 5001ms of the 5600ms failing checkout root; charge_card stays at 175ms - degradation isolated to the tax dependency

hypotheses.title: Third-party tax-api degradation saturating checkout
hypotheses.statement: tax-api stopped answering within the 5000ms client timeout from ~15:53, pinning checkout latency at the timeout ceiling and failing ~6% of checkouts; no internal change aligns with onset, consistent with a vendor-side degradation.
assumptions: telemetry in the package is complete for the window
recommended_checks: check the tax-api vendor status page and error rates from a second region
verify quote_tax durations are pinned at the client timeout rather than spread

safe_mitigation_options.rationale: runbook documents it as verified safe for up to 24h staleness with finance sign-off; bypasses the degraded vendor

safe_mitigation_options.action: Reduce the tax-api client timeout to 1500ms only together with the cached-rates fallback
safe_mitigation_options.rationale: fail fast instead of holding checkouts at 5000ms
risks: without the fallback this only makes checkouts fail faster

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-2, ongoing: checkout is degraded - about 6% of checkouts fail and p95 is pinned near 5 seconds. Evidence points at the third-party tax-api timing out (high confidence); database and card paths are healthy and no recent internal change aligns. Next: vendor status check and a second-region probe. No remediation has been executed; the cached-tax-rates fallback awaits human approval.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: checkout degradation via tax-api 2026-06-20
postmortem_draft.summary: The third-party tax-api stopped responding within our 5000ms timeout; checkout latency saturated at the timeout and ~6% of checkouts failed. The circuit breaker opened and remained open through the window.
postmortem_draft.impact: Ongoing at window end: checkout p95 5400ms (baseline 380ms), error rate 6.1% (baseline 0.4%) - direct revenue impact.
contributing_factors: possibly: 5000ms timeout too generous for a checkout-path dependency, amplifying user-visible latency
open_questions: why no cached-rates fallback was enabled automatically
action_items: open a P1 with the tax-api vendor with trace exemplars
evaluate automatic fallback to cached tax rates on circuit-open

reasoning_trace.summary: applied documented severity rules to the revenue-path degradation

combined aligned findings across sources | timing: 'Third-party tax-api degradation saturating checkout': tax-api slowness 15:53 immediately precedes checkout degradation 15:55; the only internal change is five days old
statements stay within what the cited evidence shows (4 checks, 0 non-pass)
aggregated 4 next steps deterministically (2 from hypothesis checks, 2 from missing data)
